title: Adopt xcui for ad-hoc harness UI verification (drive the sim without compiling a test)
description: Source: Axiom plugin (~/.claude/plugins/cache/axiom-marketplace/axiom/27.0.0-beta.22) — bin/xcui + skills/axiom-tools xcui-ref.md + agents/simulator-tester.md. xcui (with the AXe helper it installs via `xcui doctor --install`, brew) can tap/type/wait/assert against a RUNNING simulator app via the accessibility tree, with no XCUITest compile — same no-real-input guarantee as XCUITest (never the host mouse/keyboard). Gap in Basket: .harness/custom/visual-verify-build.md (line ~38-39 after the 2026-07-15 update) tells builders to write a TEMPORARY XCUITest just to exercise an interaction for tools/record_ui_test.sh — a full xcodebuild test cycle per throwaway visual check, easily 5-10 min each inside harness attempts. Proposed: extend visual-verify-{build,audit}.md with an xcui-based path for ONE-OFF verification (launch via build_run.sh, `axe tap --id <a11y-id>`, `xcui wait`, then simctl screenshot / recordVideo), explicitly keeping the house rule that DURABLE regression checks remain real XCUITests. Include a one-time needs-human env step (install AXe via brew; verify xcui doctor passes) and note the doc edits touch .harness/custom/** (per CLAUDE.md, any task scoped to .harness/** must be gate: needs-human — or the docs part can be done interactively while only the env step is gated). Value: high (unblocks cheap visual verification for every future views-layer harness task). Cost: medium.

title: Headless CPU profiling via xcprof + a committed performance baseline
description: Source: Axiom bin/xcprof + skills/axiom-tools xcprof-ref.md + agents/performance-profiler.md. xcprof records Instruments traces headlessly (`xcprof record --preset cpu --attach --no-prompt`), analyzes to JSON, and `xcprof compare --fail-on-regression` exits 3 on regression — a CI-gateable perf check with no Instruments GUI. Gap in Basket: zero performance coverage anywhere — no measure{}/XCTMetric in Tests/ or UITests/, no baseline, so a perf regression (e.g. emoji cascade or suggestion scoring getting slow on a large KnownItem history) would ship silently. Proposed: tools/profile_app.sh wrapping record+analyze around a fixed workload (reuse an existing XCUITest as the driver so traces are like-for-like), commit a baseline JSON, and document a compare step for perf-sensitive tasks. HONESTY CAVEAT from the plugin's own docs (xcprof-ref 'Honesty caveats'): memory/Leaks instruments are not exportable headlessly — this is CPU-only; do not oversell it as leak detection. Value: medium-high. Cost: medium.

title: Fix xcodebuild pipe-orphan risk in tools/record_ui_test.sh and tools/export_ui_screenshots.sh
description: Source: Axiom agents/test-runner.md (~line 95-96) and test-debugger.md (~line 69-70): never pipe xcodebuild through tee/grep/tail — if the pipe consumer is interrupted, the xcodebuild process is orphaned and keeps running (holding the simulator, racing later builds). Evidence in Basket: tools/record_ui_test.sh (~line 70-74, `xcodebuild test ... | tail -20`) and tools/export_ui_screenshots.sh (same pattern) both do exactly this — record_ui_test.sh was written 2026-07-15 and inherited the pattern from export_ui_screenshots.sh. An orphaned xcodebuild inside a harness attempt would also confuse the loop's cold-reset step. Proposed: redirect xcodebuild output to a log file and tail the file afterwards (or on failure), keeping exit-code propagation (mind `set -euo pipefail` interactions); parse structured results from the .xcresult instead of stdout where needed. Value: medium (real hang/corruption class, cheap fix). Cost: small.

title: Failure-only attachment export + .ips crash triage after UI-test runs
description: Source: Axiom agents/test-runner.md ('Export Failure Attachments': `xcresulttool export attachments --only-failures` + manifest.json) and agents/simulator-tester.md 'Crash Detection' / test-debugger.md Phase 3: after a failed UI-test run, sweep ~/Library/Logs/DiagnosticReports/*.ips for fresh app crashes and summarize with the bundled bin/xcsym (`xcsym crash --format=summary`). Gap in Basket: tools/export_ui_screenshots.sh always runs the WHOLE suite and exports every attachment (slow, noisy); and when a UI test fails because the app crashed, nothing locally or in the harness loop surfaces the crash reason — the builder just sees a timeout/element-not-found. Proposed: (a) add a failure-only export mode to export_ui_screenshots.sh; (b) add a post-run .ips sweep + xcsym summary step (to the script or to the harness visual-verify/audit guidance — note .harness/custom/** edits imply needs-human gating per CLAUDE.md). This is xcsym's only realistic use in this repo (on-device app, no crash aggregator, so its Sentry/ASC triage features do not apply). Value: medium. Cost: small.

title: VoiceOver focus-order regression check via xcui voiceover traverse
description: Source: Axiom bin/xcui `voiceover traverse` / `voiceover assert` (skills/axiom-tools xcui-ref.md): computes the VoiceOver announcement sequence for the current screen and diffs it against a saved expected sequence, exiting 1 on mismatch. Gap in Basket: ItemRow.swift (~lines 90-115) deliberately engineers VoiceOver structure ('One VoiceOver stop for the row's info, separate from the check button') and AddBar/QuantityEditor carry curated labels/hints, but UITests/AccessibilityAuditTests.swift (performAccessibilityAudit) cannot assert focus ORDER or announcement PHRASING — a regression that scrambles row reading order or merges stops would pass today's audits. Proposed: a small script (tools/) that launches the seeded app on the simulator, runs `xcui voiceover traverse`, and asserts against a committed expected-sequence file; wire into CI or the DoD for views-layer tasks. Depends on the same AXe/xcui environment step as the xcui ad-hoc verification idea — cluster them at conversion time. Value: medium. Cost: small-medium.

title: Capture app console output (xclog) during visual verification
description: Source: Axiom bin/xclog (skills/axiom-tools xclog-ref.md): `xclog launch <bundle-id> --timeout --max-lines --filter <regex>` captures print + os.Logger output from a simulator app headlessly. Gap in Basket: the app logs through three Logger subsystems (BasketApp/KnownItems persistence errors, TipJar), but build_run.sh discards the console entirely — a failed SwiftData save or intent error during a harness build's visual verification is invisible; the screenshot looks fine while persistence silently failed. Proposed: optional log-capture step in build_run.sh (or the visual-verify guidance) using xclog with a `(?i)error|fault` filter, attaching the output to the worklog when non-empty. Note: guidance edits touch .harness/custom/** (needs-human gate per CLAUDE.md). Value: low-medium. Cost: small.

title: Add element-not-found triage discipline to the harness audit guidance
description: Source: Axiom agents/test-debugger.md 'Element Not Found Triage': when a UI test fails to find an element, screenshot the live state first — if the element is genuinely absent from the screen, it is an APP bug; never 'fix' the test by loosening/rewriting selectors to make it pass. Gap in Basket: .harness/custom/visual-verify-audit.md judges rendered pixels but has no rule preventing an unattended builder from responding to a failing accessibility-identifier query by rewriting the query (or the identifier) until the test passes, masking a real regression — a plausible cheap-model failure mode the harness audit should catch. Proposed: add the triage rule to visual-verify-audit.md and/or .harness/custom/audit-preamble.md (both plugin-overlay files under .harness/custom/, so the task needs gate: needs-human per CLAUDE.md — or do it interactively). Value: low-medium (cheap insurance against a known unattended-agent failure mode). Cost: small.

title: Skip production app startup when the app is hosted by unit tests
description: Source: Axiom skills/axiom-testing swift-testing.md Strategy 3 + agents/testing-auditor.md section 2.1: a unit-test host app that runs its full production init wastes seconds per run and touches real state; guard with NSClassFromString("XCTestCase") (or equivalent) to swap in inert startup. Evidence in Basket: Sources/BasketApp.swift (~lines 25-57) — when BasketTests runs, the host app launches WITHOUT -uiTesting, so init() opens the REAL App Group store, seeds starter items, registers fonts, and instantiates TipJar; it even fatalError()s if the container fails. The unit tests themselves build in-memory containers, but the host still pays (and mutates) production startup on every test invocation — including inside every harness attempt's LOCAL_DOD run. Proposed: add an isRunningTests check (in TestHooks, consistent with the existing switchboard) that swaps in an in-memory store and skips seeding/font registration/TipJar when hosted by XCTest without -uiTesting; verify unit-test wall time drops and no UITest behavior changes. Value: medium. Cost: small.

title: Extract pure logic into a BasketCore SwiftPM package; retire tools/main.swift
description: Source: Axiom skills/axiom-testing swift-testing.md Strategy 1: move pure logic into a SwiftPM package so `swift test` runs it in ~0.4s with a real test framework (parameterized @Test(arguments:), per-case reporting) instead of a hand-rolled harness. Evidence in Basket: tools/main.swift (247 lines) hand-rolls a check() DSL with manual failure counting, duplicating assertions that already exist in Tests/BasketTests.swift and friends; .github/workflows/ci.yml (~lines 80-88) must hand-list every source file on a swiftc invocation — drift-prone (the file's own header admits it exists as a workaround). Emoji/Suggestions/Measure/Seasonality/ListLogic/Formatting are already pure and dependency-free. Proposed: create a BasketCore package housing the pure services, consumed by the app target via XcodeGen; replace the native-harness step in CI + DoD docs with `swift test`; convert table-style suites (emoji table, measure classification) to parameterized tests. NOTE from the same research: a general XCTest-to-Swift-Testing migration was judged NOT worth doing standalone (UITests must stay XCTest; snapshot/StoreKitTest suites gain nothing) — the parameterized-test win should ride along with this extraction, not be its own task. Large change touching project.yml, CI, CLAUDE.md, README. Value: medium. Cost: large.

title: Bounded stays-absent assertion helper for UI tests (assertStaysGone)
description: Source: Axiom skills/axiom-testing ui-testing/xctest-automation guidance: never assert element existence/absence as an instantaneous read — absence right now does not mean absence after the UI settles. Evidence in Basket: five XCTAssertFalse(...exists) call sites violate BasketUITestCase's own 'never assert live UI state directly' house rule — worst is UITests/DeepLinkTests.swift:67 (testNormalLaunchDoesNotAutoFocus asserts the dismiss button doesn't exist immediately after the field appears; focus arriving a beat later would falsely PASS); lower-risk but same shape at CheckOffFlowTests.swift:77 and :127, RenameFlowTests.swift:38, PersistenceFlowTests.swift:67 (each follows a correlated bounded wait). Proposed: add an assertStaysGone(_:for:) helper to BasketUITestCase (poll for a short settle window, fail if the element ever appears) and route those five call sites through it; document in the base class comment block. Value: medium (closes a false-pass window in the determinism story). Cost: small.

title: Make the nightly flake-hunt actionable: auto-file/update a GitHub issue
description: Source: Axiom's failure-analysis discipline (agents/test-failure-analyzer.md) presumes flake DETECTION feeds a fix loop — detection that lands in an artifact nobody opens is dead signal. Evidence in Basket: .github/workflows/ci.yml's scheduled flake-hunt job (reruns every test 5x nightly) only uploads the failing .xcresult as an artifact — no notification, no tracking; meanwhile the push-triggered job's retry-once (-retry-tests-on-failure -test-iterations 2) actively MASKS the same flakes on merges, so a repeatedly-flaky test can rot indefinitely with no human ever seeing it. Proposed: on flake-hunt failure, parse the failing test identifiers from the xcresult (xcrun xcresulttool) and `gh issue create` (or update a single pinned 'flaky tests' issue) with the test names + run link. Keeps the existing tolerance policy; just stops it being silent. Note: touches .github/workflows/ci.yml (build layer). Value: medium. Cost: small.

title: Extract the Clear-all/undo soft-delete choreography into ListLogic (unit-tested)
description: Source: Axiom agents/swiftui-architecture-auditor.md Phase 4: multi-step async business logic living raw in a view body with no unit tests is its top-severity compound finding. Evidence in Basket: Sources/Views/ShoppingListView.swift (~lines 29-34 state, ~383-425 logic) — pendingClearIDs/clearToken/showClearToast plus token-invalidated DispatchQueue.asyncAfter deletion form a second state machine (begin/undo/expire with stale-token guards) embedded in the view, and it performs the app's only DESTRUCTIVE operation (permanent deletes). The repo already proved the right pattern on the other state machine: CheckOffChoreography lives in Sources/Services/ListLogic.swift (~50-79) as a pure, unit-tested value type. Proposed: extract a ClearChoreography value type into ListLogic (same style), unit-test the races (expire-after-undo, double-clear superseding a pending expiry, undo-after-expiry no-op), leave the view holding only the timer scheduling and context.delete calls. IMPORTANT SEQUENCING: backlog task T038 (already pending) modifies commitClear() to fix the flicker bug — this extraction should depend on / land AFTER T038 so they don't collide on the same lines; the extraction must preserve T038's narrowed-buffer semantics. Value: high. Cost: medium.

title: Small SwiftUI/services cleanups: AnyView plumbing, dead GeometryReader, Haptics.prepare
description: Bundle of three tiny independent cleanups surfaced by Axiom's swiftui-performance-analyzer, swiftui-layout-auditor, and energy-auditor against Basket. (1) AnyView type erasure: ShoppingListView.editor(for:) returns AnyView? (~line 293-307) threaded through ToGetSection into ItemRow.quantityEditor: AnyView? (ItemRow.swift:31) — defeats SwiftUI structural identity/diffing; impact is small (one expanded row at a time) but the fix is mechanical: make ItemRow generic over the editor view or construct the editor inside ToGetSection. (2) Dead GeometryReader: Sources/Theme/Theme.swift (~103-108) BasketBackground captures geo.size into w/h that dots() never uses — Canvas receives its own size; pure deletion. (3) Haptics.prepare() (Sources/Services/Haptics.swift) is never called anywhere — either dead code to delete or a missing pre-warm call before the first check-off haptic; decide and do one. All three are behavior-preserving; snapshot tests should stay green unmodified. Value: low-medium. Cost: small.

title: Idle the 60s ticker when nothing needs it + add timer tolerance
description: Source: Axiom agents/energy-auditor.md Pattern 1 (timers should carry ~10% tolerance so the OS can coalesce wakeups; timer-driven whole-view invalidation should be conditional) + swiftui-performance-analyzer (the .animation(value: items.map(\.isChecked)) at ShoppingListView.swift ~100-103 re-allocates that array on every tick-driven redraw). Evidence in Basket: ShoppingListView.swift:49 — `Timer.publish(every: 60, on: .main, in: .common)` runs forever, forcing a full list re-render every minute even when the Got-it section is empty and `now` changes nothing user-visible (the ticker exists only to age out recentlyGot items against their 1-hour TTL and refresh Seasonality flourishes). Proposed: (a) add tolerance (Timer.publish(every:tolerance:on:in:) — e.g. 6s); (b) connect the ticker only while recentlyGot is non-empty (or a seasonality window is active), disconnecting otherwise — keeping all time reads routed through AppClock.now per the TestHooks determinism rules, and verifying the UI tests' frozen-clock behavior is unaffected. Foreground-only app so the energy win is modest; the redraw-churn win is the realer half. Value: low-medium. Cost: small.

title: Anchor the shipped SwiftData schema: VersionedSchema V1 + migration plan + container-failure recovery
description: Source: Axiom agents/swiftdata-auditor.md Phase 3 + skills/axiom-data swiftdata-migration.md: an app shipped WITHOUT a VersionedSchema has no anchor for SwiftData's migration machinery — any future rename/type-change/unique-constraint addition becomes an unplanned lightweight migration that the simulator hides (store deleted on rebuild) and real devices hit cold: worst case a crash-loop with 'incompatible model' on every launch. Evidence in Basket: zero VersionedSchema/SchemaMigrationPlan/migrationPlan hits anywhere in Sources/ or Tests/; containers are built ad hoc at Sources/BasketApp.swift (~26-48) and Sources/Services/AddToBasketIntent.swift (~80-81); GroceryItem.swift:19's own comment acknowledges only additive changes are currently safe. This app is LIVE on the App Store — its users' installed stores are the released schema forever. Second half (same task, per the auditor's 'recovery path' check): container-open failure is currently fatalError at BasketApp.swift:50 and Support/AppGroup.swift:21, i.e. a failed future migration = permanent crash loop; instead, on container-init throw, move the store aside (Basket.store.broken-<date>), recreate fresh, and log — an acceptable data-loss tradeoff for a grocery list vs. a bricked app. Done-when sketch: BasketSchemaV1 (GroceryItem + KnownItem) + a one-version SchemaMigrationPlan passed at every container init; a unit test that opens a fixture .store copied from the released schema and migrates it; recovery path unit-tested with a corrupt store file. Value: HIGH (protects real users' data on a shipped app). Cost: small-medium.

title: Single shared SwiftData schema/container factory across app, intent, and widget
description: Source: Axiom agents/swiftdata-auditor.md cross-process checks: when multiple targets/processes open the same store file with independently-declared model sets, a model added to one site but not another means two processes disagree on the schema over one SQLite file — invisibility or incompatible-store failures. Evidence in Basket: the model list [GroceryItem.self, KnownItem.self] is independently spelled out at FOUR call sites today — three branches in BasketApp.init (in-memory, UITEST_STORE_URL, App Group) and one in AddToBasketIntent.resolveContainer — and pending task T033 (widget extension) adds a FIFTH in a new target/process. Adding a third @Model to only some sites is a realistic future-task failure mode the harness's cold builders would walk right into. Proposed: an AppSchema enum (static models list) + makeSharedContainer()/makeInMemoryContainer() factory in Sources/Support/, adopted at all existing call sites; T033's spec should then reference the factory instead of hand-building its own container (T033 spec edit touches .harness/tasks/, so either amend before the loop picks it up or gate that part needs-human). Sequencing: ideally lands BEFORE T033 builds. Value: HIGH. Cost: small.

title: Amend T033 widget spec: cross-process freshness (WidgetCenter reload) + read-only container
description: Source: Axiom skills/axiom-data (grdb-app-groups section 7 — conceptually applies to any shared-SQLite setup including SwiftData) + skills/axiom-integration WidgetKit guidance: a reader process NEVER sees another process's writes automatically; the writing process must nudge the widget via WidgetCenter.shared.reloadTimelines(ofKind:) after saves, and the widget should treat its container as read-only per timeline fetch. Evidence in Basket: .harness/tasks/T033.md says only 'Keep the timeline refresh cadence sensible (re-read on a reasonable interval / on app writes)' — no mechanism named, and T033's scope reads widget-side-only, but the reload calls belong APP-side: after context.save() in BasketApp's scenePhase-background flush, after add/check-off/clear mutations, and in AddToBasketIntent.perform() (Siri-add while the widget is on screen is the headline staleness case). Without this, the shipped widget shows stale data for arbitrary periods and the cold builder has no instruction to prevent it. Proposed: amend T033's spec (## Do + ## Done when + scope, which must grow to cover the app-side files) BEFORE the loop builds it — a .harness/tasks/ + TASKS.json scope edit, so per CLAUDE.md do it interactively or as a needs-human task. Also fold in: verify the widget target's required-reason API usage (App Group UserDefaults, file timestamps) is covered by PrivacyInfo.xcprivacy, per the security agent's T033 note. Value: HIGH (prevents shipping a stale-by-design widget). Cost: small.

title: Respect Reduce Motion for the per-item SparkleBurst
description: Source: Axiom agents/accessibility-auditor.md Pattern 7 (WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions): decorative motion should be suppressed under Reduce Motion; withAnimation does not auto-respect it. Evidence in Basket: Sources/Views/ItemRow.swift:102 plays SparkleBurst() on EVERY check-off unconditionally — the app's single most frequent animation — while the codebase already gates its two rarer bursts correctly (ClearedCelebration.swift:14 and LaunchFlourish.swift:31 both check reduceMotion), so this is an inconsistency, not a missing convention. TestHooks' withAppAnimation only disables animation under UI testing, not under the accessibility setting. Proposed: read @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) in ItemRow (or pass it down like ClearedCelebration does), skip the burst overlay when set (keep the check-state change + haptic), and note the check circle's own spring still animates via .unlessUITesting — decide whether that spring also calms under Reduce Motion for consistency with the other two call sites. UI-test impact: none expected (bursts aren't asserted). Value: medium. Cost: small.

title: Dynamic Type middle ground: scale the pixel fonts via relativeTo
description: Source: Axiom agents/accessibility-auditor.md Patterns 2-4: Font.custom(name, size:) WITHOUT relativeTo: freezes text at every Dynamic Type setting; the documented remedy Font.custom(_:size:relativeTo:) keeps a custom typeface AND scales with the user's setting — i.e. Basket's 'pixel fonts are deliberately fixed-size' stance (the documented reason UITests/AccessibilityAuditTests.swift excludes .dynamicType) is a false dichotomy per the plugin: the aesthetic and scaling are not mutually exclusive. Evidence: Sources/Theme/Theme.swift (~75-80) builds every app font as .custom(name, size: size * scale) or .system(size:) with no relativeTo anywhere. Proposed: thread relativeTo: (mapping each usage to .body/.title etc.) through Theme's font helpers; test at accessibility sizes (consider capping via .dynamicTypeSize(...(.accessibility2)) if layout genuinely breaks at AX5 — the plugin endorses capped scaling over none); re-record snapshot references; re-enable the .dynamicType audit category in AccessibilityAuditTests (removing the exclusion + its justification comment). Also unlocks honestly declaring Larger Text support in the Accessibility Nutrition Labels idea. Real accessibility win for a shipped app. Value: high. Cost: medium-large (layout + snapshot fallout is exactly why it was deferred originally).

title: Swift 6 strict-concurrency migration (before the widget lands)
description: Source: Axiom agents/concurrency-auditor.md Phases 3/5: mutable unisolated statics and nonisolated(unsafe) without migration notes are its top flags; readiness is scored by counting escape hatches. Evidence in Basket: project.yml:20 pins SWIFT_VERSION "5.0" with no strict-concurrency flag; the actual violation surface is SMALL — LaunchFlourish.swift:9 (nonisolated(unsafe) static var fired) and AddToBasketIntent.swift:64 (static var containerOverride, a mutable unisolated global that is a guaranteed Swift 6 error); everything else is already @MainActor (TipJar) or value types. Timing argument: pending task T033 adds a widget EXTENSION — a second process compiling shared Sources/ files — which is exactly when latent concurrency assumptions start biting; migrating while the surface is 2 statics is the cheap moment. Proposed: enable -strict-concurrency=complete, fix the two statics (@MainActor annotation or isolated alternatives), chase any remaining diagnostics, then flip SWIFT_VERSION to 6.0 in project.yml; full suite green. Sequencing: ideally before or alongside T033. Value: medium. Cost: small-medium.

title: Tip jar: surface pending (Ask to Buy) and failed purchases in the UI
description: Source: Axiom agents/iap-auditor.md Pattern 11 (purchase errors mapped to actionable user feedback; .pending — Ask to Buy / SCA — must not look like nothing happened). Evidence in Basket: Sources/Services/TipJar.swift (~102-109) — the purchase() switch handles .pending by silently breaking, and the catch block deliberately swallows errors; a child tapping the tip on an Ask-to-Buy account just sees the spinner stop with zero acknowledgment (the Transaction.updates listener at ~line 42 does eventually record an approved purchase, so nothing is LOST — this is feedback-only polish). The auditor's other checks all pass (verify-then-finish on both paths, app-lifetime updates listener, displayPrice, consumable-so-no-restore). Proposed: track a lightweight status on TipJar (@Observable) — e.g. .pendingApproval / .failed(transient message) — and render a quiet one-line note in AboutView's tip section. House rule: NO em dashes in the user-facing copy. Value: low. Cost: small.

title: Declare Accessibility Nutrition Labels in App Store Connect (needs-human)
description: Source: Axiom skills/axiom-shipping app-store-submission checklist: Accessibility Nutrition Labels are the (new-ish) App Store Connect metadata surface where an app declares which accessibility features it supports (VoiceOver, Larger Text, Reduce Motion, etc.); already-shipped apps should fill them in — it is store-visibility metadata, not code. Evidence in Basket: the app has genuinely strong, audit-TESTED VoiceOver support (AccessibilityAuditTests + curated labels/hints/identifiers throughout) that is declarable TODAY, but nothing has been declared in ASC. Dependencies: cannot honestly claim Larger Text until the Dynamic Type relativeTo idea lands; the Reduce Motion claim strengthens if the SparkleBurst gating idea lands first. This is an App Store Connect console task requiring the owner's account — author as gate: needs-human (no facets per harness rules), with the code-side ideas as dependsOn if converted together. Value: medium (store visibility + honest signaling). Cost: small (human console work).
